title: Aurora Galactica And Platinum Project Guide
strapline: A current, hosted map of the project: what Platinum is, what each game owns, how ingestion and conformance work, how release lanes move, and which maintained docs are the source of truth.
currentGoal: Keep production trustworthy on the live 1.4.0 public family while shaping the next deliberate follow-through: improve Aurora's Galaga-like gameplay/audio/visual quality, keep Platinum clean as the reusable host, and keep turning ingestion plus local harnessing into a repeatable path for future games.

cards.title: What Shipped
cards.body: `1.4.0` is now the current public Aurora / Platinum production family. Hosted `/dev` remains the visible `1.4.0.1` forward-review line, while hosted `/beta` remains the approved `1.4.0-beta.1` production-source lane and the release path keeps the shell, picker, release dashboards, and hosted docs part of the product.

cards.title: What The Platform Owns
cards.body: Platinum owns the shell, release framing, hosted lanes, pack selection path, shared services, conformance dashboard surfaces, and first-class hosted documentation that explain the platform and release model.

cards.title: What The Application Owns
cards.body: Each game owns its own gameplay truth. Aurora owns scoring, stage flow, challenge cadence, capture and rescue, dual-fighter behavior, enemy behavior, visual/audio identity, and Aurora-specific conformance evidence.

cards.title: Current Focus
cards.body: The main work is now post-1.4.0 follow-through: audio/event feedback, level arc, boss and formation grammar, visual reference grounding, resource economics, and first-class playable/conformance advancement for Galaxy Guardians and future games.

sections.summary: Shareable lane-relative links for Aurora, Galaxy Guardians, and the combined dashboard view.

sections.title: Conformance Program
sections.summary: How reference evidence becomes measurable software quality.

cards.title: Ingestion
cards.body: Ingestion gathers source footage, clips, manifests, contact sheets, waveforms, event logs, semantic annotations, and confidence notes. It is the evidence front-end for conformance, not a loose design notebook.

cards.title: Harnessing
cards.body: Harnesses run local CPU/browser checks against reference-derived targets: movement, timing, audio, stage shape, boss formation grammar, visual surfaces, pressure windows, and shell behavior.

cards.title: Metrics
cards.body: Metrics include score, confidence, resolution, evidence links, target gaps, user-experience meaning, and cost to move. A 10/10 score means maxed at current scorer resolution, not perfect imitation.

cards.title: Resource Economics
cards.body: Local CPU and browser runs should carry repeated measurement. Codex/OpenAI/model work is tracked as GPU-equivalent when it materially shapes strategy, harnesses, code, or analysis.

sections.title: Release Ladder
sections.summary: How work is expected to move through the system.
bullets: local `localhost` is the active engineering lane and the source of the current candidate.
hosted `/dev` is the shared integration preview and currently carries the visible `1.4.0.1` forward-review increment.
hosted `/beta` is the approved `1.4.0-beta.1` production-source lane and the staging point for the next deliberate review cycle.
hosted `/production` is the public promise and now carries the live `1.4.0` public family.
A meaningful `x.y` release should now include a complete documentation pass before hosted `/production` promotion.

sections.title: Platform Versus Applications
sections.summary: The architectural split we need to preserve.

cards.title: Platinum
cards.body: Platinum owns shared shell framing, runtime hosting, pack selection, shared services, release tooling, and the hosted documentation and release surfaces that apply to more than one game.

cards.title: Aurora Galactica
cards.body: Aurora is the first shipped application on Platinum. It owns game rules, challenge and scoring behavior, capture and rescue, and Aurora-specific content and gameplay harnesses.

cards.title: Galaxy Guardians
cards.body: Galaxy Guardians is currently a playable-preview sibling application on Platinum. Its current job is to prove second-game hosting, game-owned identity, and first-class conformance growth without pretending the experience is already fully mature.

sections.title: Galaxy Guardians Stage Assessment
sections.summary: A human-readable read of what Guardians currently is, what the stage arc should feel like, and what work best advances it toward a deeper first-class game.

cards.title: Current visible truth
cards.body: Hosted `dev` and `beta` still present a one-level visible public slice that ends with `mission_complete`. That slice still needs stronger `WAIT` presentation, score-table fidelity, moving starfield motion, march cadence, explosion clarity, reserve ships, flags, missile-ready cues, and visible top reentry before we should treat it as a convincingly mature second cabinet.

cards.title: Deeper internal game shape
cards.body: Under the public slice, the repo already has a repeated-rack and stage-band model: stages `1-2` establish the rack and fair early dives, stages `3-5` escalate into scout-to-flagship pressure, stages `6-9` become a sustained session, and stages `10+` act as a bounded late-loop review band instead of pretending to be a finished infinite campaign.

cards.title: Best next work
cards.body: The highest-return sequence is: finish the opening-slice baseline from the committed Galaxian sources, promote stage `3-5` and `6-9` motion and pressure artifacts, add real Aurora-versus-Guardians platform-frame parity scoring, and only then open the door to homage variants.

cards.title: Variant guardrail
cards.body: Future homage variants should preserve single-shot discipline, a marching rack, scout-to-flagship command pressure, visible bottom-exit/top-reentry threat, score-table literacy, and sparse arcade audiovisual identity. If a variant loses those, it may still be good, but it is no longer truly Guardians/Galaxian-family.

links.label: Read Full Guardians Stage-Arc Plan
links.detail: Full rendered stage-by-stage narrative, work progression, metric strategy, and homage-variant guardrails.

links.label: Open Guardians Conformance View
links.detail: Current score, gaps, and next investment areas for Guardians on the hosted dashboard.

sections.title: Automation-First Gating
sections.summary: The release process should prefer automation over memory or manual estimation whenever practical.
bullets: Build and publish preflight should fail if the hosted docs set is incomplete.
Gameplay, shell, and platform boot behavior should be covered by focused harnesses whenever the behavior is stable enough to automate.
Hosted lane verification should confirm live labels and, when relevant, live input behavior.
Manual review should mostly be reserved for feel, visual quality, and presentation clarity where automation is still weaker.

sections.title: Supabase Data API Contract
sections.summary: How Platinum keeps auth, profile, and leaderboard database access reproducible after the Supabase public-schema grant change.

cards.title: Current Runtime Surface
cards.body: The browser Data API currently uses `public.scores` for shared and validated leaderboards plus score submission, and `public.profiles` for signed-in pilot initials.

cards.title: Owned Access Artifact
cards.body: `supabase/data-api-access-contract.sql` records explicit grants, RLS posture, and policy names for the current tables. `SUPABASE_DATA_API_ACCESS.md` explains the product meaning and release risk.

cards.title: Release Gate
cards.body: `npm run harness:check:supabase-data-api-contract` compares runtime `supabase.from(...)` table usage against the SQL contract. Publish preflight runs the same check.

cards.title: Future Feature Rule
cards.body: Any new Supabase table for YouTube high-score posting, replay-video metadata, richer pilot records, or later games must land with explicit grants and policies before runtime feature work can publish.

sections.title: Security, Auth, And Replay Storage Lock-Down
sections.summary: The release-facing rule set for account, score, replay, and future high-score video posting work.

cards.title: Posting Boundary
cards.body: Top-10 video posting is authenticated and authorized only. The browser may prompt a top-10 player to sign in, but uploads require a future server-owned path.

cards.title: No Browser Secrets
cards.body: No Supabase service-role material, OAuth client secret, YouTube upload token, or upload endpoint may ship in browser code. Arcade Music remains a playlist embed, not upload authority.

cards.title: Replay Storage
cards.body: Replay video remains browser-local IndexedDB by default. A score can link to a local replay on the same device, but hosted replay/video posting needs explicit storage, auth, and RLS rules.

cards.title: Release Gate
cards.body: `npm run harness:check:security-auth-replay-storage` is now part of publish preflight alongside the Supabase Data API contract gate.

sections.title: Likely Next Steps
sections.summary: The work that most naturally follows the current shipped state.
bullets: Use the conformance dashboard to choose the highest-value Aurora investment by score gap, user impact, confidence, and resource cost.
Prioritize audio/event feedback, level arc, boss/formation grammar, and visual reference grounding for the next major user-experience lift.
Keep Platinum shell and docs discipline boring and reliable while the games become more ambitious.
Advance Galaxy Guardians through ingestion-backed reference evidence before treating it as a serious public playable candidate.
